title: Pouteria longicuneata
taxonomy: Pouteria longicuneata is classified within the family Sapotaceae, which consists of over 900 species of trees and shrubs. This family is part of the order Ericales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Pouteria itself is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas and Africa. Pouteria longicuneata is a species of flowering plant endemic to Ecuador. Further classification of Pouteria longicuneata involves its classification into the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Pouteria longicuneata, also known as the Brazilian cherry or babacu, is a species of tree in the family Sapotaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The wood and seeds of the Brazilian cherry are used in traditional medicine, where they have been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria longicuneata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The seeds and wood of the Brazilian cherry are commonly prepared into herbal supplements, including capsules and tinctures, and are also used to make teas and infusions.
